item.title: UK power shares fall after government flags price shake-up
item.description: SSE, Centrica and Orsted among those hit after Reeves says country will look to break link between power and gas prices

item.title: Judge orders GE Vernova to stay on Vineyard offshore wind project
item.description: Order issued Friday enjoins US wind turbine manufacturer from stopping work on ‘forever first’ array developed by Spain's Iberdrola and Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners as dispute goes through court

item.title: Judge appears open to blocking GE Vernova from abandoning Iberdrola’s Vineyard Wind
item.description: Massachusetts judge casts doubt on GE Vernova's right to abandon Vineyard Wind but delays ruling on temporary restraining order

item.title: Deals of the Week: Equinor, Amazon, RWE and Envision
item.description: Norway's Equinor sold off shares in renewable energy developer Scatec, leading to speculation it could be precursor to jettisoning stake in offshore wind giant Orsted

item.title: EDP parks US offshore wind projects to 'wait for better days' as rivals retreat
item.description: Developer with Engie in 2.4GW SouthCoast array signals notable optimism as TotalEnergies gives up on sector, with RWE expected to follow

item.title: Four key arguments GE Vernova has raised in Vineyard Wind dispute
item.description: US wind turbine manufacturer says offshore wind Iberdrola-Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners project company is trying to 'rewrite contract' and that alleged harm should it quit work would be 'self-inflicted'

item.title: UK Supreme Court's Orsted ruling a setback for wind and solar, say experts
item.description: Ruling comes at a time when cost pressures and financing constraints are already making life difficult in building new wind farms and solar arrays, say legal experts

item.title: Why China wants its clean tech champions to calm down
item.description: Battery leader CATL ramps up its capacity guidance despite government efforts to dampen competition in the sector

item.title: First US state passes data centre ban over power fears – but the reality is complex
item.description: Steep power price hikes are often blamed on AI, but Maine has not seen large loads come online and its 55% rate hike is more linked to natural gas shortfalls

item.title: Chinese foundations 'drive down European wind costs', but one supplier wants to go further
item.description: Chinese foundations have already carved out a share of European offshore wind market and now Dajin Heavy Industry wants to offer a one-stop shop services including transport and installation

item.title: Is Orsted next on the block as Equinor sells renewables stake?
item.description: Analysts ponder implications for oil giant's holding in Danish offshore wind group as it divests half of its shares in Scatec

item.title: GE Vernova hits back in Vineyard court battle
item.description: US turbine manufacturer claimed 'forever first' project owes it money and is using litigation to gain commercial advantage

item.title: Offshore wind to power Britain's railways
item.description: Network Rail to take power from RWE wind farm under new power deal that marks further uptick for European sector

item.title: Orsted: 'UK court ruling damages business case for offshore wind'
item.description: Supreme Court ruled that Orsted could not claim tax relief for surveys it carried out developing offshore wind farms in UK, a decision with implications for all renewable energy projects

item.title: Spain gets cosy with China over batteries and floating wind
item.description: Agreement highlights Spain’s openness to Chinese clean-tech investment

item.title: Lower offshore wind target could cost Germany tens of thousands of jobs: study
item.description: Wind at sea already employs over 31,000 people in Europe’s largest economy, but rise to 120,000 jobs by 2045 depends on expansion ambition

item.title: Cutting German offshore wind target will have painful consequences: study
item.description: Wind at sea already employs over 31,000 people in Europe’s largest economy, but rise to 120,000 jobs by 2045 depends on expansion ambition

item.title: UK Supreme Court rules against Orsted in landmark case for renewables
item.description: Supreme Court favours narrow interpretation of what costs project developers can claim tax relief for, with implications for wind, solar and other industries

item.title: UK Supreme Court rules against Orsted in landmark renewables dispute
item.description: Supreme Court favours narrow interpretation of what costs project developers can claim tax relief for – in ruling with implications for wind, solar and other industries

item.title: Why the US wind industry is caught in a polling paradox
item.description: Recent surveys indicate that Republicans support either renewable energy or fossil fuels depending on framing, with issues of affordability trumping the environment

item.title: UK energy minister hits back at Scottish leader in Mingyang row
item.description: Scotland’s First Minister Swinney had decried move by UK government bar turbines from China's Mingyang over national security concerns

item.title: Orsted braces for UK Supreme Court ruling with major implications for wind
item.description: Supreme Court ruling concerning four offshore wind farms will affect the tax payable for surveys during development of all kinds of renewable energy projects

item.title: Chinese government says UK Mingyang decision will damage relations
item.description: UK barred Mingyang turbines from offshore wind farms in British waters, citing concerns over national security

item.title: Equinor sells stake in renewables developer amid shift away from wind and solar
item.description: Oil and gas giant halves holding in Norwegian company after previously lowering capacity target for renewables

item.title: Nordex Q1 wind turbine orders fall while prices rise slightly
item.description: CEO Blanco sees 'healthy order pipeline' in Europea and North America for the full year

item.title: Chinese OEMs top wind turbine deliveries to dominate global market
item.description: China's manufacturers emphasised scale, but Western OEMs saw healthy boosts too by focusing on value and price discipline, said consultancy Wood Mackenzie

item.title: 'World's largest' wind plus solar plant now pumping power
item.description: Envison Energy says 6GW mega-plant, boasting a mix of wind farms and solar farms, is operating in remote mountainous northwest China

item.title: Six key claims in Iberdrola's lawsuit against GE Vernova
item.description: Spanish renewable energy giant Iberdrola has slammed GE Vernova's work on huge offshore wind project, claiming things started poorly and became ‘catastrophic’

item.title: Massive battery blaze raises new safety questions
item.description: More than 80 tonnes of batteries went up in flames in incident likely to turn new spotlight on industry's safety record

item.title: Iberdrola dispute with GE Vernova over US offshore wind farm erupts into open
item.description: Dispute stems from fallout of blade breaking off GE Vernova wind turbine at Vineyard Wind project in 2024, which led to huge replacement programme and extensive delays
